name: Homemade Slice & Bake Oatmeal Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup raisins, or up to 2 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut four pieces of waxed paper or plastic wrap that are 14 inches by 12 inches. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, cream together the butter or margarine and both sugars until smooth. Beat in vanilla extract and eggs until light and fluffy.
3. In a medium bowl, combine flour, oats, salt, baking soda, and baking powder. Stir this mixture into the egg and butter mixture; blend well. Mix in raisins and nuts evenly.
4. Divide the dough into four portions. Shape each portion into a roll that is 8 inches to 10 inches long. Wrap each roll in waxed paper.
5. Place the rolls in a large freezer container with a tight-fitting lid, or seal them in a large freezer zip-lock bag, or wrap them well in heavy-duty aluminum foil. Label with date and contents.
6. Store in the freezer. Use within 6 months.
7. To prepare cookies: Slightly thaw one roll of oatmeal cookie dough. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
8. Cut the dough into slices that are about 1/4 inch thick. Place the slices on lightly greased cookie sheets, spacing them about 1 inch apart. Bake for about 10-12 minutes, or until the centers are slightly set and the edges are lightly browned.
9. Remove from the oven and let cool for 2 minutes on the baking sheet before transferring to wire racks to cool completely. Makes about 3 dozen cookies.
10. Makes 4 rolls of cookie dough, which can yield about 12 dozen cookies.
